parsed_questions.question_text: A) Rank in order of SN2 reactivity (1= most reactive) four alkyl iodides shown: benzyl iodide, 1-iodo-2-methylpropane, 1-iodobutane, iodobenzene. B) Rank in order of leaving group ability (1= best leaving group) for: NH2-, H2O, Cl-, I-. C) Rank in order of nucleophilicity (1= best nucleophile) for: PhCH2S-, PhCH2O-, PhCH2OH, PhCO2-. D) Rank in order of alkene stability (1= most stable) for the four alkenes from left to right as shown. E) Rank in order of radical stability (1= most stable) for four radicals: methylcyclopentyl radical, cyclopentyl radical, allylcyclopentyl radical, vinylcyclopentyl radical. F) Select the best solvent for an SN2 reaction from two shown: acetone (selected) and methanol.

Draw the structure of the products of the following reactions. Indicate stereochemistry where appropriate. If there is no reaction, write NR. A) Bicyclo[1.1.1]pentane with two bromines and one hydrogen reacting with NaCN in DMF. B) Methyl substituted cyclopentane with an unspecified leaving group reacting with azide ion (N3-) in DMF. C) Ethylene chloride (ClCH2CH2O) reacting with H2O. D) 1-chlorobutane reacting with H2O. E) Cyclohexylmethyl chloride reacting with potassium tert-butoxide (t-BuOK) in tert-butanol (t-BuOH).
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) Bicyclo[1.1.1]pentane with Br and CN groups (3-exo-cyano-bicyclo[1.1.1]pentane) with bromine and cyano substituents shown (structure drawn). B) Methylcyclopentane with azide substituent (azidomethylcyclopentane). C) Ethylene glycol (HOCH2CH2OH). D) NR. E) Cyclohexene (unsaturated cyclohexane, Hoffman elimination product).

parsed_questions.error_comment: Task requires drawing chemical structures and is not text-oriented

parsed_questions.question_text: One of the reaction coordinate energy diagrams corresponds to an SN1 mechanism, the other corresponds to an SN2 mechanism. Indicate which reaction coordinate energy diagram goes with which mechanism. Two diagrams shown: Left diagram shows a single peak; right diagram shows two peaks with an intermediate plateau. Instructions: Circle one: SN1 SN2 for left diagram; Circle one: SN1 SN2 for right diagram.
Draw the structures of A and B. Starting from cyclohexane, Br2 and hv give product A. Then A reacts with NaCN in DMF to yield product B. Provide the structures of A and B.

Draw the structures of the starting materials and reagents (A, B, C) needed to synthesize the given product 1-propanol. The synthesis involves: A reacts with Mg in ether to give B; then B reacts with reagent C followed by H2O to provide 1-propanol. The reagent C is placed in a box placeholder {box_1}.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) CH3CH2Br; B) CH3CH2MgBr; C) Formaldehyde (HCHO)

parsed_questions.question_text: Show the synthesis of the following product in two steps from methylcyclopentane by drawing the structures of A, B, C. Starting material: methylcyclopentane. Reaction sequence: methylcyclopentane → A → B → C → product (cyclopentene).
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A=Br2, hv; B=structure of bromomethylcyclopentane; C=MeO or OtBu substitution

parsed_questions.question_text: Show the synthesis of the following product in two steps by drawing the structures of the starting materials and reagents (A, B, C, D) for the following reaction. Hint: A is an alkyne. Reaction sequence: A → B → C → D → product (1-butyne).

parsed_questions.question_text: A) Select the reaction that is more regioselective between: 1) bromination of a straight-chain alkane with Br2, hv resulting in a single brominated product shown, 2) chlorination of the same alkane with Cl2, hv which produces multiple chlorinated products. B) Select the reaction that is more regioselective between: 1) allylic bromination of 1-butene with NBS, hv showing resonance structures indicating different radical intermediates, 2) allylic bromination of cyclopentadiene with NBS, hv showing resonance structures and a single brominated product on the ring.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) bromination with Br2, hv; B) allylic bromination of cyclopentadiene with NBS, hv

parsed_questions.question_text: Circle the heterocycles that are aromatic. Structures shown are: 1) pyridinium cation (N+ in a six-member ring), 2) thiophene, 3) borole (five-membered ring with B), 4) thiophene-like with 3 double bonds, 5) thiazole (N and S in five-member ring), 6) imidazole (N and NH in five-membered ring), 7) purine-like structure (two fused rings with N and NH and NH2 substituent).

parsed_questions.question_text: The compound shown below (structure of a bicyclic compound with nitrogen atoms) is not aromatic. Explain why not. Draw a picture or a model if this will help explain your reasoning.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: There are 2 nitrogen atoms that will be too close – molecule must bend to avoid steric clash. It is cyclic, has 4n+2 electrons, but is not planar.
